title: Log In Or Log Out From The VISU_DATA Screen
summary: Use the VISU_DATA screen on the operator station HMI to log in to the operator station or log out the currently logged-in user, then verify the displayed user ID reflects the intended session state.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when an operator needs to change the active user session at the operator station using the VISU_DATA screen LOGIN or LOGOUT controls and verify the displayed current user ID.
not_for: Do not use this runbook for credential-entry instructions or alternate login methods; the source does not provide those details.
Do not use this runbook for maintenance or admin-only parameter changes; the source packet does not support those actions here.

responsibilities: Navigate to the VISU_DATA screen on the operator station HMI.
Use the LOGIN or LOGOUT button as needed.
Verify the displayed current user ID reflects the intended session state.

safety_notes: This source describes an HMI interaction and does not specify lockout/tagout requirements.
Do not assume credential prompts, alternate login paths, or additional controls beyond those shown on the VISU_DATA screen.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the operator station HMI

access_required: Access to the operator station HMI
system_preconditions: The operator station HMI is available.
The VISU_DATA screen can be opened or selected.
external_references: Figure 4-20 Operator Station HMI Data Screen

visual_references.description: VISU_DATA screen showing the current logged-in user ID and the LOGIN and LOGOUT buttons.
Operator station display controls showing how to navigate to the Visu_Data screen.
Additional VISU_DATA screen reference showing the data screen layout.

steps.title: Open the VISU_DATA screen
steps.purpose: Reach the HMI screen that contains the login, logout, and current user ID display.
steps.instruction: Open or navigate to the VISU_DATA screen on the operator station HMI.
steps.expected_result: The VISU_DATA screen is displayed on the operator station HMI.
steps.healthy_condition: The VISU_DATA screen is visible and includes the current user ID field and LOGIN and LOGOUT buttons.
steps.failure_condition: The VISU_DATA screen cannot be reached or the expected controls are not visible.

actions.label: Navigate to VISU_DATA
actions.exact_action: Open or navigate to the VISU_DATA screen on the operator station HMI.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Screen-navigation controls and the Visu_Data mapping used to access the VISU_DATA screen.
Overall VISU_DATA screen layout, including the LOGIN button, LOGOUT button, and current user ID field.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the VISU_DATA screen cannot be opened.
Stop or escalate if the expected LOGIN, LOGOUT, or current user ID display is not present.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The Next Screen drop-down menu, the buttons on the right side of the screen, and the physical buttons on the right side of the display navigate to the same screens. The listed control mapping includes F5 to "Visu_Data."
This operator station screen includes a display of the currently logged-in user ID, and LOGIN and LOGOUT buttons.

Press the LOGIN button to log in to the operator station. Press the LOGOUT button to log out the user currently logged in to the operator station. The screen displays the ID of the user currently logged in.

steps.title: Locate the current user ID display
steps.purpose: Identify the field used to verify the active session before and after login or logout.
steps.instruction: Locate the displayed current user ID on the VISU_DATA screen.
steps.expected_result: The operator can identify the user ID field that shows the currently logged-in user.
steps.healthy_condition: A user ID display is visible on the VISU_DATA screen.
steps.failure_condition: The current user ID field is not visible or cannot be identified.

actions.label: Find current user ID
actions.exact_action: Locate the displayed current user ID.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The display area showing the ID of the user currently logged in.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the current user ID field cannot be found on the VISU_DATA screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The screen displays the ID of the user currently logged in.
This operator station screen includes a display of the currently logged-in user ID.

steps.title: Press LOGIN to log in
steps.purpose: Initiate operator station login from the VISU_DATA screen.
steps.instruction: To log in, press the LOGIN button on the VISU_DATA screen.
steps.expected_result: The operator station begins or completes the login action through the LOGIN control.
steps.healthy_condition: The LOGIN button is present and responds to operator input.
steps.failure_condition: Pressing LOGIN does not change the displayed session state as expected.

actions.label: Press LOGIN
actions.exact_action: Press the LOGIN button to log in to the operator station.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The LOGIN button and nearby current user ID display on the VISU_DATA screen.
Additional VISU_DATA screen context for the data screen layout.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if pressing LOGIN does not change the displayed session state as expected.
Stop or escalate if additional credential-entry details are required, since the source does not provide them.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Press the LOGIN button to log in to the operator station.
This operator station screen includes LOGIN and LOGOUT buttons.

steps.title: Verify the logged-in user ID
steps.purpose: Confirm that the login action resulted in the intended active session.
steps.instruction: Verify that the user ID display updates to show the currently logged-in user.
steps.expected_result: The current user ID field reflects the active logged-in user.
steps.healthy_condition: The displayed user ID matches the intended logged-in session.
steps.failure_condition: The displayed user ID does not update or does not match the intended logged-in session.

actions.label: Verify logged-in user
actions.exact_action: Verify that the user ID display updates to show the currently logged-in user.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The current user ID field after pressing LOGIN.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the displayed user ID does not update after pressing LOGIN.
Stop or escalate if the displayed user ID does not match the intended session state.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The screen displays the ID of the user currently logged in.

steps.title: Press LOGOUT to log out the current user
steps.purpose: End the current operator session from the VISU_DATA screen.
steps.instruction: To log out the current user, press the LOGOUT button on the VISU_DATA screen.
steps.expected_result: The operator station begins or completes logout of the currently logged-in user.
steps.healthy_condition: The LOGOUT button is present and responds to operator input.
steps.failure_condition: Pressing LOGOUT does not change the displayed session state as expected.

actions.label: Press LOGOUT
actions.exact_action: Press the LOGOUT button to log out the user currently logged in to the operator station.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The LOGOUT button and nearby current user ID display on the VISU_DATA screen.
Additional VISU_DATA screen context for the data screen layout.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if pressing LOGOUT does not change the displayed session state as expected.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Press the LOGOUT button to log out the user currently logged in to the operator station.
This operator station screen includes LOGIN and LOGOUT buttons.

steps.title: Verify the user session display after logout
steps.purpose: Confirm that the logout action changed the displayed session state.
steps.instruction: Verify the user session display after logout by checking the current user ID field.
steps.expected_result: The current user ID field reflects the post-logout session state.
steps.healthy_condition: The displayed session state after logout matches the intended result on the current user ID field.
steps.failure_condition: The current user ID field does not change after logout or still shows an unexpected session state.

actions.label: Verify logout state
actions.exact_action: Verify the user session display after logout by checking the current user ID field.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The current user ID field after pressing LOGOUT.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the current user ID field does not change after pressing LOGOUT.
Stop or escalate if the displayed session state remains inconsistent with the intended logout result.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The screen displays the ID of the user currently logged in.
Escalate if pressing LOGIN or LOGOUT does not change the displayed session state as expected.

success_criteria: The VISU_DATA screen is accessible on the operator station HMI.
The LOGIN and LOGOUT controls are available on the VISU_DATA screen.
The current user ID field can be viewed.
After login or logout, the displayed user ID reflects the intended session state.
healthy_conditions: The VISU_DATA screen shows the current logged-in user ID.
The LOGIN button can be used to log in to the operator station.
The LOGOUT button can be used to log out the currently logged-in user.
The displayed user ID matches the active session state.
failure_conditions: The VISU_DATA screen cannot be opened.
The LOGIN or LOGOUT button is not visible or does not respond as expected.
The current user ID field cannot be identified.
Pressing LOGIN or LOGOUT does not change the displayed session state as expected.
Credential-entry details are needed but are not provided by the source.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if pressing LOGIN or LOGOUT does not change the displayed session state as expected.
Escalate if the VISU_DATA screen or its current user ID field is unavailable.
Escalate if additional credential-entry or alternate login-path details are required, because the source does not provide them.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Press the LOGIN button to log in to the operator station. Press the LOGOUT button to log out the user currently logged in to the operator station. The screen displays the ID of the user currently logged in.

missing_details: The source does not provide credential-entry details for the LOGIN action.
The source does not describe the exact post-logout display state beyond using the current user ID field for session indication.
The source does not provide timing expectations for login or logout completion.
The source does not specify whether production must be stopped before performing this action.
The source does not specify lockout/tagout requirements for this HMI action.
